2604.12518.title: Enhance-then-Balance Modality Collaboration for Robust Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
2604.12518.paper_summary: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) integrates heterogeneous text, audio, and visual signals to infer human emotions. While recent approaches leverage cross-modal complementarity, they often struggle to fully utilize weaker modalities. In practice, dominant modalities tend to overshadow non-verbal ones, inducing modality competition and limiting overall contributions. This imbalance degrades fusion performance and robustness under noisy or missing modalities. To address this, we propose a novel model, Enhance-then-Balance Modality Collaboration framework (EBMC). EBMC improves representation quality via semantic disentanglement and cross-modal enhancement, strengthening weaker modalities. To prevent dominant modalities from overwhelming others, an Energy-guided Modality Coordination mechanism achieves implicit gradient rebalancing via a differentiable equilibrium objective. Furthermore, Instance-aware Modality Trust Distillation estimates sample-level reliability to adaptively modulate fusion weights, ensuring robustness. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EBMC achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results and maintains strong performance under missing-modality settings.
2604.12518.paper_summary_zh: 多模態情感分析（MSA）整合異質的文本、音頻和視覺信號來推斷人類情感。雖然最近的方法利用了跨模態的互補性，但它們往往難以充分利用較弱的模態。在實踐中，主導模態往往會掩蓋非語言模態，導致模態競爭並限制整體貢獻。這種不平衡會降低在噪音或缺失模態下的融合性能和穩健性。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新模型，增強後平衡模態協作框架（EBMC）。EBMC通過語義解耦和跨模態增強來提高表示質量，增強較弱的模態。為了防止主導模態壓倒其他模態，能量引導的模態協調機制通過可微平衡目標實現隱式梯度重平衡。此外，實例感知模態信任蒸餾估計樣本級可靠性，以自適應地調整融合權重，確保穩健性。大量實驗表明，EBMC達到了最先進或具有競爭力的結果，並在缺失模態設置下保持強大的性能。
2604.12518.author: Kang He et.al.
2604.12518.authors: Kang He, Yuzhe Ding, Xinrong Wang, Fei Li, Chong Teng, Donghong Ji
